title: emerging markets / sovereign spreads / foreign exchange reserves | Blog Post | A Closer Look at Emerging Market Resilience During Recent Sh...
summary: New York Fed researchers analyze the divergent resilience of emerging market economies during recent global shocks, finding that "Core" EMs (22 MSCI Index countries) have substantially stronger buffers and better outcomes than "Periphery" EMs (92 countries outside the index). Core EMs share of global GDP doubled from 14% to 33% over 25 years, while Periphery EMs remained flat at 6%. During risk-off episodes, Periphery EM sovereign spreads increased far more than Core EM spreads, with Periphery median spreads rising 45 bps to 332 bps since the Middle East conflict onset.
